title: Fluentd Memory Buffer Utilization Approaching Limit (Pre-Overflow Detection) (fill-rate slope, minutes-to-full forecast, Fluent Bit storage gauges, Splunk Connect for Kubernetes hec paths, Calyptia builds, tcpin pressure feedback)
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.7.1 post-overflow buffer saturation and retry exhaustion analytics, UC-13.7.4 worker crash and supervisor restart detection, per-index Splunk indexer queue capacity gold cards in isolation, or automatic modification of Fluentd configuration. Does not assert data loss has occurred; it forecasts pressure using slope proxies that require human validation during non-linear traffic.
evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_7_5_buffer_fill_forecast with five-to-ten minute schedule; platform logging dashboard drilldowns on multisearch lanes; alert routing to Head of Platform delegated on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab logging namespace, throttle a non-production Splunk HTTP Event Collector token to induce rising fluentd_output_status_buffer_total_bytes while scrapes continue, confirm slope_bytes_per_min turns positive with est_minutes_to_full falling below twenty minutes for fifteen minutes of sustained samples, and verify observability_uc_13_7_5_buffer_fill_forecast returns critical or high with non-null recommended_action.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After restoring throughput when Prometheus shows stable buffer gauges near baseline and Fluent Bit storage busy bytes flatten, confirm severities return to low or medium and filtering to critical yields zero rows for thirty consecutive scheduled runs.

dataSources: (index=fluentd OR index=fluent_bit OR index=kubernetes OR index=metrics OR index=platform OR index=_internal) sourcetype=fluentd:metrics Prometheus exposition with fluentd_output_status_buffer_total_bytes fluentd_output_status_buffer_queue_length fluentd_output_status_buffer_stage_length; sourcetype=fluent_bit:metrics with fluentbit_storage_chunks fluentbit_storage_chunks_busy_bytes; sourcetype=fluentd:logs warn-level buffer pressure foreshadow lines; kubelet prometheus or otel_metrics with container_memory_working_set_bytes memory_rss for logging DaemonSets; index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*splunktcpin* optional ingest pressure; lookup fluentd_output_inventory.csv keyed on output_id; cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata for pipeline_region maint_window
app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud + fluent-plugin-prometheus or monitoring agent + Splunk Connect for Kubernetes (optional) + Calyptia Fluent Bit or Fluentd enterprise builds (optional)
spl:
`comment("UC-13.7.5 proactive buffer fill-rate forecast Fluentd Fluent Bit Calyptia SCK; tune window slopes and sustain gates per fleet earliest=-4h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes.node_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,plugin_type,plugin,"fluentd_out")))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,match_output_id,plugin_id)))
      | eval metric=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric,metric_name,name,""))))
      | eval q_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(buffer_total_queued_size,buffer_total_bytes,fluentd_output_status_buffer_total_bytes,value,_value,"0")),10)
      | eval q_len=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(buffer_queue_length,fluentd_output_status_buffer_queue_length,queue_length,"0")),10)
      | eval stg_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(buffer_stage_byte_size,fluentd_output_status_buffer_stage_length,stage_bytes,"0")),10)
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=coalesce(if(q_bytes>0,q_bytes,null()),if(stg_bytes>0,stg_bytes,null()),q_len*65536)
      | eval chunk_lim=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(chunk_limit_size,"67108864")),10)
      | eval total_limit_size=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(total_limit_size,total_limit,fluentd_buffer_total_limit,"268435456")),10)
      | eval queue_limit_length=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(queue_limit_length,"512")),10)
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=min(buffer_used_bytes+stg_bytes,total_limit_size)
      | eval output_p95_flush_latency_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(output_time_flush_time_count,flush_time_sum_ms,flush_latency_ms,"0")),10)
      | eval retry_count_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retry_count,fluentd_output_status_retry_count,"0")),10)
      | eval environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,deployment_env,"production"))))
      | fields _time host plugin_id output_id buffer_used_bytes total_limit_size queue_limit_length q_len output_p95_flush_latency_ms retry_count_recent environment ]
    [ search index=fluent_bit sourcetype=fluent_bit:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,pod_name,kubernetes.pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin,input_name,output_name,"fluent_bit_output")))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,alias,name,plugin_id)))
      | eval busy_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentbit_storage_chunks_busy_bytes,storage_busy_bytes,value,_value,"0")),10)
      | eval chunks=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentbit_storage_chunks,storage_chunks,"0")),10)
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=coalesce(if(busy_bytes>0,busy_bytes,null()),chunks*98304)
      | eval total_limit_size=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_total_limit_bytes,storage_max_chunks_up_limit,"134217728")),10)
      | eval queue_limit_length=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_max_chunks_up,"128")),10)
      | eval q_len=chunks
      | eval output_p95_flush_latency_ms=round(tonumber(tostring(coalesce(task_duration_seconds,output_duration_seconds,"0")),10)*1000,2)
      | eval retry_count_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retries_failed,retry_failed_total,"0")),10)
      | eval environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,deployment_env,"production"))))
      | fields _time host plugin_id output_id buffer_used_bytes total_limit_size queue_limit_length q_len output_p95_flush_latency_ms retry_count_recent environment ]
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"buffer is full|buffer full|queue full|slow flush|high.?water|throttl|back.?pressure|retry wait|waiting buffer")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval plugin_id=coalesce(plugin_id,"fluentd_warn_foreshadow")
      | eval output_id=coalesce(output_id,"unmapped_output")
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(inferred_buffer_bytes,"88000000")),10)
      | eval total_limit_size=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(inferred_limit_bytes,"104857600")),10)
      | eval queue_limit_length=256
      | eval q_len=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(warn_queue_len,"48")),10)
      | eval output_p95_flush_latency_ms=4500
      | eval retry_count_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(warn_retries,"4")),10)
      | eval environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"production"))))
      | fields _time host plugin_id output_id buffer_used_bytes total_limit_size queue_limit_length q_len output_p95_flush_latency_ms retry_count_recent environment ]
    [ search (index=kubernetes OR index=metrics OR index=platform) earliest=-4h@h latest=now (sourcetype=kubelet_metrics OR sourcetype=prometheus OR sourcetype=otel_metrics)
      | eval rawm=lower(_raw)
      | where match(rawm,"fluent|fluent-bit|fluentd") AND match(rawm,"memory_working_set|memory_rss|container_memory")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,kubernetes.pod_name,pod,name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval plugin_id="kube_shipper_mem"
      | eval output_id="daemonset_logging_agent"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?<mv>\d{7,13})"
      | eval mem_metric=tonumber(mv,10)
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=round(mem_metric*0.62,0)
      | eval total_limit_size=round(mem_metric*0.95,0)
      | eval queue_limit_length=128
      | eval q_len=0
      | eval output_p95_flush_latency_ms=0
      | eval retry_count_recent=0
      | eval environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"production"))))
      | fields _time host plugin_id output_id buffer_used_bytes total_limit_size queue_limit_length q_len output_p95_flush_latency_ms retry_count_recent environment ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (source=*splunktcpin* OR source=*SplunkTcp*)
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval plugin_id="splunk_tcpin_pressure"
      | eval output_id="hec_ingest_path"
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(current_queue_size,queue_size,kb,"0")),10)
      | eval total_limit_size=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(max_queue_size,queue_limit,"8000000")),10)
      | eval queue_limit_length=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(active_connections,connections,"0")),10)
      | eval q_len=queue_limit_length
      | eval output_p95_flush_latency_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(wait_ms,latency_ms,"0")),10)
      | eval retry_count_recent=0
      | eval environment="platform_ingest"
      | fields _time host plugin_id output_id buffer_used_bytes total_limit_size queue_limit_length q_len output_p95_flush_latency_ms retry_count_recent environment ]
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval plugin_id=coalesce(plugin_id,"unknown_plugin")
| eval output_id=coalesce(output_id,"unknown_output")
| eval queue_length=coalesce(q_len,0)
| eval merge_key=strcat(host,"|",output_id,"|",plugin_id)
| sort 0 merge_key + _time
| streamstats window=30 current=t global=f first(_time) AS t0 first(buffer_used_bytes) AS v0 last(_time) AS t1 last(buffer_used_bytes) AS v1 BY merge_key
| eval delta_sec=coalesce(t1,0)-coalesce(t0,0)
| eval slope_bytes_per_min=if(delta_sec>1, round((coalesce(v1,0)-coalesce(v0,0))/(delta_sec/60),4), 0)
| eval buffer_used_bytes=coalesce(v1,buffer_used_bytes)
| eval buffer_used_pct=round(100*buffer_used_bytes/nullif(total_limit_size,0),3)
| eval buffer_headroom_bytes=coalesce(total_limit_size,0)-coalesce(buffer_used_bytes,0)
| eval slope_fill=case(slope_bytes_per_min>0,slope_bytes_per_min, true(),1)
| eval est_minutes_to_full=if(slope_bytes_per_min>0, round(buffer_headroom_bytes/slope_fill,2), 99999)
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(buffer_used_pct>80 AND est_minutes_to_full<15,1,0))) AS sustain_hi BY merge_key
| eventstats perc95(buffer_used_bytes) AS env_buffer_p95 avg(buffer_used_bytes) AS env_buffer_avg BY environment
| sort 0 merge_key - _time
| dedup merge_key
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [| inputlookup fluentd_output_inventory.csv
     | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,output_key,dest_code)))
     | fields output_id destination owner_team criticality on_call_runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
      | dedup output_id
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
      | rename maint_window AS pipeline_maint_window
      | table output_id pipeline_region pipeline_maint_window ]
| eval destination=coalesce(destination,pipeline_region,"unmapped_destination")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval criticality=coalesce(criticality,"medium")
| eval severity=case(sustain_hi>=5 AND buffer_used_pct>80 AND est_minutes_to_full<15,"critical", buffer_used_pct>78 OR (est_minutes_to_full<20 AND slope_bytes_per_min>2048000),"high", buffer_used_pct>65 OR est_minutes_to_full<40 OR env_buffer_p95>buffer_used_bytes*0.85,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","page_oncall_expand_buffer_raise_flush_threads_validate_hec_token_and_disk_inode_budget", severity=="high","increase_flush_thread_count_shorten_flush_interval_trace_429_tls_and_chunk_limit_size", severity=="medium","schedule_capacity_review_rebalance_outputs_and_calibrate_exporter_cardinality", true(),"observe_trend_confirm_maintenance_windows_and_baseline_slopes")
| table _time, host, plugin_id, output_id, destination, owner_team, criticality, buffer_used_bytes, total_limit_size, buffer_used_pct, slope_bytes_per_min, est_minutes_to_full, queue_length, output_p95_flush_latency_ms, severity, recommended_action
description: Detects rising Fluentd and Fluent Bit buffer utilization before hard overflow by fusing Prometheus buffer byte and queue gauges, Fluent Bit storage busy bytes, warn-level engine foreshadow logs, Kubernetes memory pressure proxies for shipper pods, and optional Splunk tcpin introspection, then computes sliding slope_bytes_per_min with streamstats, estimates est_minutes_to_full from headroom, enforces sustained high-risk gates, and enriches rows with output inventory and pipeline metadata for each destination.
value: Platform and SRE teams gain lead time when log volume storms or downstream throttles begin filling buffers but have not yet exhausted retries, letting them rebalance flush threads, chunk sizes, and Splunk HTTP Event Collector capacity before customer-visible log gaps form. Finance and risk stakeholders receive traceable evidence that capacity management watched leading indicators separately from post-failure overflow analytics.
implementation: Land fluentd:metrics Prometheus scrapes, fluent_bit:metrics storage gauges, fluentd:logs foreshadow warnings, kubelet or prometheus pod memory series, and optional _internal splunktcpin; normalize output_id; schedule observability_uc_13_7_5_buffer_fill_forecast every five to ten minutes with earliest=-4h@h; join fluentd_output_inventory.csv and cmdb fluentd_pipeline_metadata; route critical and high severities to platform logging on-call; archive weekly evidence exports.
visualization: Severity-colored table by host and output_id with sparklines for buffer_used_pct and slope_bytes_per_min, single-value tiles for est_minutes_to_full, drilldowns to raw metrics JSON, foreshadow logs, and Splunk tcpin lines when enabled.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.mem) AS mem_latest latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS shipper_host
| join type=left max=0 shipper_host [| inputlookup fluentd_output_inventory.csv | eval shipper_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,node,"")))) | fields shipper_host destination owner_team]
| where cpu_load>0 OR isnotnull(mem_latest)
| table shipper_host mem_latest cpu_load destination owner_team
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries for hosts and containers that run Fluentd or Fluent Bit when memory and CPU fields are mapped; accelerate Inventory summaries when output destinations are modeled as configuration items tied to output_id; retain thirty days for quarterly platform attestations.

references.title: Fluent Bit Documentation — Buffering and Storage
Splunk Connect for Kubernetes — GitHub Pages

knownFalsePositives: Planned ingest tier upgrades can raise Splunk tcpin queue signals and shipper slopes together without customer impact when change tickets annotate pipeline_maint_window; require correlation before paging. Scheduled batch backfills of historical logs intentionally refill buffers; gate alerts using job labels and replay identifiers. Downstream Splunk maintenance windows may slow acknowledgements and inflate flush latency; cross-check indexer health dashboards. Dev and test clusters idle near zero traffic produce noisy slope estimates when denominators approach zero; filter environments or raise minimum byte floors. Low-traffic workers warming after deploy show transient spikes; require sustained_hi before critical pages. Canary clusters mirroring production cardinality during dress rehearsals can mimic production fill without user traffic; tag rehearsal namespaces. Certificate renewal drills may pause TLS briefly causing slope blips; validate against PKI calendars. Mis-labeled output_id values break inventory joins and fabricate unmapped_destination rows; refresh fluentd_output_inventory.csv weekly. Prometheus target drops can zero an arm while others rise; monitor scrape success separately. Duplicate metric labels after chart upgrades can double-count bytes until dedupe transforms land; validate gauge cardinality after helm bumps.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode analytics on output_id entities by pipeline_region and owner_team

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the observability pipeline team that operates Fluentd open source collectors, Fluent Bit DaemonSets, Calyptia Enterprise or Splunk-distributed Fluent builds, Splunk Connect for Kubernetes logging agents, and legacy Treasure Data td-agent packages still under extended support contracts. This use case is deliberately proactive: it estimates how quickly per-output buffers are approaching configured byte and queue ceilings using sliding windows, linear fill slope proxies, and minutes-to-full math before chunks block emit paths or spill into error storms. It complements UC-13.7.1, which focuses on post-overflow retry exhaustion and saturated queues after failure modes have hardened, and UC-13.7.4, which focuses on worker crashes and supervisor-driven respawn loops where process death can empty queues in misleading ways. When UC-13.7.5 fires alongside UC-13.7.1, treat the pair as a staged narrative: here you still have headroom and time to intervene; there the system already lost leeway and is shedding or stalling. When UC-13.7.5 fires with UC-13.7.4, separate memory pressure leading toward out-of-memory kills from pure buffer math by reading Kubernetes memory metrics arm rows and kube event timelines before you blame downstream Splunk HTTP Event Collector throttles alone.

Fluentd buffers isolate bursty producers from slower consumers. The buffer section of an output plugin chooses memory or file backing. Memory buffers minimize latency and disk wear yet vanish on restart unless paired with additional durability strategies. File buffers trade latency for persistence and multiply inode pressure on root volumes or dedicated persistent volumes. The buffer directives that matter for capacity planning include flush_interval, which sets periodic flush cadence; flush_thread_count, which parallelizes network writers; chunk_limit_size, which caps per-chunk bytes; total_limit_size, which caps aggregate bytes retained; queue_limit_length, which caps queued chunk count; and retry_max_times, which bounds how long the engine retries before giving up. Mis-tuning any one directive changes whether this search sees gradual fill versus sudden cliffs. Calyptia-hardened Fluentd builds and Splunk Connect for Kubernetes helm charts often ship opinionated defaults for hec outputs: validate whether your cluster uses the stock chart values or forked stanzas that inflate chunk_limit_size to reduce object count at the cost of slower flush granularity.

Monitoring posture differs between classic in_monitor_agent polling of Ruby Fluentd and modern Prometheus text exposition. Version one style agents expose JSON via loopback HTTP on port 24220 for /api/plugins.json. Version two monitoring agents and prometheus scrapes emit counters and gauges such as fluentd_output_status_buffer_total_bytes, fluentd_output_status_buffer_queue_length, and fluentd_output_status_buffer_stage_length. Normalize exporter label drift with coalesce ladders so the same search survives renames across minor gem upgrades. Fluent Bit exposes fluentbit_storage_chunks and fluentbit_storage_chunks_busy_bytes when storage.type filesystem backs the engine, while memory-only modes emphasize mem_buf_limit pressure visible through scheduler metrics and warn logs. Splunk Connect for Kubernetes maps hec destinations into output identifiers you should align with fluentd_output_inventory.csv rows so paging routes to the team that owns the destination contract.

RBAC and secrets remain sensitive. Scrapers need service accounts that reach pod metrics endpoints inside kube-system or your logging namespace without granting cluster-admin. Splunk-side tcpin introspection in _internal is platform-operator data; restrict roles so application teams cannot read production cluster topology casually. Redact raw log samples in pager templates when foreshadowing arms surface message excerpts that might embed customer payloads.

Index design: land sourcetype=fluentd:metrics for Prometheus-style scrapes, sourcetype=fluent_bit:metrics for Fluent Bit JSON or Prometheus lanes, sourcetype=fluentd:logs for engine warnings that precede hard overflow, kubelet or prometheus sourcetypes for container_memory_working_set_bytes and memory_rss proxies, and sourcetype=splunkd with splunktcpin sources for receiver pressure feedback. Maintain fluentd_output_inventory.csv with output_id, destination URI family, owner_team, criticality, and on_call_runbook_url. Maintain cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata with pipeline_region and maint_window to suppress benign fills during approved work.

Risk briefing: minutes-to-full estimates assume roughly linear fill over the trailing window. Burst traffic that is piecewise constant will mis-estimate when slopes flatten between bursts. Always pair slope math with retry_count_recent and output_p95_flush_latency_ms to catch nonlinear stalls caused by TLS handshakes or rate limiting. Legal reviewers should approve retention for internal splunktcpin logs when used outside classic platform teams.

Differentiation recap: proactive fill percentage, headroom bytes, slope_bytes_per_min from streamstats first-and-last regression, est_minutes_to_full, sustained high-risk counts, inventory enrichment, pipeline metadata join, multisource Fluentd Fluent Bit kube memory Splunk tcpin lanes, not post-overflow exhaustion storytelling from UC-13.7.1, not supervisor crash loops from UC-13.7.4.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five multisearch arms Splunk can fuse without assuming a single exporter always works during incidents.

Arm Fluentd Prometheus metrics: enable the fluent-plugin-prometheus gem or the monitoring agent that exposes output buffer gauges. Scrape endpoints with a Prometheus server or a Splunk HTTP Event Collector collector that normalizes metric labels into metric, plugin_id, and output_id fields. Map fluentd_output_status_buffer_total_bytes into buffer_used_bytes semantics, map fluentd_output_status_buffer_queue_length into queue_length, and map fluentd_output_status_buffer_stage_length into stage bytes before bytes aggregate. Include cluster, namespace, pod, and node labels for Kubernetes-hosted Fluentd. For virtual machines running td-agent under systemd, include host and availability zone tags.

Arm Fluent Bit metrics: turn on HTTP or Prometheus metrics compatible with your security posture. Persist storage.type and storage.path in configuration management snapshots so analysts know whether busy bytes imply disk or memory pressure. Forward fluentbit_storage_chunks and fluentbit_storage_chunks_busy_bytes on the same interval as Fluentd scrapes to keep slopes comparable.

Arm Fluentd logs for foreshadowing: ship /var/log/td-agent/td-agent.log or container stdout with multiline-aware LINE_BREAKER so slow flush warnings remain intact. Capture warn-level lines that mention buffer pressure, throttling, or retry waits before BufferOverflowError lines dominate in UC-13.7.1 investigations.

Arm Kubernetes pod resource metrics: scrape kubelet summary API metrics, Prometheus kube-state-metrics cadvisor series, or OpenTelemetry host metrics pipelines that include container_memory_working_set_bytes and memory_rss for fluent-bit and splunk-connect pods. Restrict to logging namespaces to control cardinality.

Arm Splunk receiver introspection: allow index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source patterns for splunktcpin when governance permits. This arm provides crude feedback when indexer-side queues grow while shipper buffers also swell, hinting shared downstream pressure.

props.conf guidance: honor millisecond timestamps on Prometheus scrapes, set TRUNCATE high for wide metric lines, and strip ANSI color from container logs. transforms.conf can copy kubernetes.pod_name into host when host is empty for DaemonSets.

Pre-save validation: each arm must return events in lab within one hour of enabling scrapes. Induce a harmless throttle on a non-production HEC token and confirm slope_bytes_per_min turns positive while retry_count_recent climbs.

Example operator helpers for design reviews:

```bash
curl -sS localhost:24220/api/plugins.json | head
curl -sS localhost:2020/api/v1/metrics | head
kubectl -n logging get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/name=fluent-bit -o wide
kubectl -n logging top pod -l k8s-app=splunk-kubernetes-logging
```

Dashboards: heatmap host by output_id for buffer_used_pct, sparkline slope_bytes_per_min, single-value est_minutes_to_full for top offenders, overlay Splunk tcpin queue hints when the fifth arm is enabled.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_7_5_buffer_fill_forecast with a five-minute schedule in dense fleets and a ten-minute schedule when Prometheus cardinality or multisearch cost requires breathing room, using dispatch earliest=-4h@h and latest=now. Page when severity is critical, warn when high, and route medium to platform chat with owner_team in the title. Throttle duplicate host, output_id pairs for twenty minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager text so on-call engineers open runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans Fluentd metrics, Fluent Bit storage metrics, Fluentd warn foreshadow logs, Kubernetes memory proxies, and Splunk tcpin feedback. coalesce absorbs missing host, plugin_id, and output_id labels. streamstats window=30 computes trailing slope_bytes_per_min using first and last buffer_used_bytes over time per merge_key as a linear slope-per-minute approximation. eventstats adds environment baselines env_buffer_p95 for hotspot context. streamstats window=5 sums indicator events where buffer_used_pct exceeds eighty and est_minutes_to_full drops below fifteen minutes to approximate sustained pressure. case maps combined sustained stress, fill percentage, slope, and baseline comparisons into critical, high, medium, and low tiers. join enriches output_id with fluentd_output_inventory.csv and cmdb fluentd_pipeline_metadata for destination and maintenance hints.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.7.5 proactive buffer fill-rate forecast Fluentd Fluent Bit Calyptia SCK; tune window slopes and sustain gates per fleet earliest=-4h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,kubernetes.node_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin_id,plugin_type,plugin,"fluentd_out")))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,match_output_id,plugin_id)))
      | eval metric=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric,metric_name,name,""))))
      | eval q_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(buffer_total_queued_size,buffer_total_bytes,fluentd_output_status_buffer_total_bytes,value,_value,"0")),10)
      | eval q_len=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(buffer_queue_length,fluentd_output_status_buffer_queue_length,queue_length,"0")),10)
      | eval stg_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(buffer_stage_byte_size,fluentd_output_status_buffer_stage_length,stage_bytes,"0")),10)
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=coalesce(if(q_bytes>0,q_bytes,null()),if(stg_bytes>0,stg_bytes,null()),q_len*65536)
      | eval chunk_lim=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(chunk_limit_size,"67108864")),10)
      | eval total_limit_size=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(total_limit_size,total_limit,fluentd_buffer_total_limit,"268435456")),10)
      | eval queue_limit_length=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(queue_limit_length,"512")),10)
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=min(buffer_used_bytes+stg_bytes,total_limit_size)
      | eval output_p95_flush_latency_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(output_time_flush_time_count,flush_time_sum_ms,flush_latency_ms,"0")),10)
      | eval retry_count_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retry_count,fluentd_output_status_retry_count,"0")),10)
      | eval environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,deployment_env,"production"))))
      | fields _time host plugin_id output_id buffer_used_bytes total_limit_size queue_limit_length q_len output_p95_flush_latency_ms retry_count_recent environment ]
    [ search index=fluent_bit sourcetype=fluent_bit:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,pod_name,kubernetes.pod_name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval plugin_id=trim(toString(coalesce(plugin,input_name,output_name,"fluent_bit_output")))
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,alias,name,plugin_id)))
      | eval busy_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentbit_storage_chunks_busy_bytes,storage_busy_bytes,value,_value,"0")),10)
      | eval chunks=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(fluentbit_storage_chunks,storage_chunks,"0")),10)
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=coalesce(if(busy_bytes>0,busy_bytes,null()),chunks*98304)
      | eval total_limit_size=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_total_limit_bytes,storage_max_chunks_up_limit,"134217728")),10)
      | eval queue_limit_length=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(storage_max_chunks_up,"128")),10)
      | eval q_len=chunks
      | eval output_p95_flush_latency_ms=round(tonumber(tostring(coalesce(task_duration_seconds,output_duration_seconds,"0")),10)*1000,2)
      | eval retry_count_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(retries_failed,retry_failed_total,"0")),10)
      | eval environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,deployment_env,"production"))))
      | fields _time host plugin_id output_id buffer_used_bytes total_limit_size queue_limit_length q_len output_p95_flush_latency_ms retry_count_recent environment ]
    [ search index=fluentd sourcetype=fluentd:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where match(lr,"buffer is full|buffer full|queue full|slow flush|high.?water|throttl|back.?pressure|retry wait|waiting buffer")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval plugin_id=coalesce(plugin_id,"fluentd_warn_foreshadow")
      | eval output_id=coalesce(output_id,"unmapped_output")
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(inferred_buffer_bytes,"88000000")),10)
      | eval total_limit_size=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(inferred_limit_bytes,"104857600")),10)
      | eval queue_limit_length=256
      | eval q_len=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(warn_queue_len,"48")),10)
      | eval output_p95_flush_latency_ms=4500
      | eval retry_count_recent=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(warn_retries,"4")),10)
      | eval environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"production"))))
      | fields _time host plugin_id output_id buffer_used_bytes total_limit_size queue_limit_length q_len output_p95_flush_latency_ms retry_count_recent environment ]
    [ search (index=kubernetes OR index=metrics OR index=platform) earliest=-4h@h latest=now (sourcetype=kubelet_metrics OR sourcetype=prometheus OR sourcetype=otel_metrics)
      | eval rawm=lower(_raw)
      | where match(rawm,"fluent|fluent-bit|fluentd") AND match(rawm,"memory_working_set|memory_rss|container_memory")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,kubernetes.pod_name,pod,name,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval plugin_id="kube_shipper_mem"
      | eval output_id="daemonset_logging_agent"
      | rex field=_raw max_match=1 "(?<mv>\d{7,13})"
      | eval mem_metric=tonumber(mv,10)
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=round(mem_metric*0.62,0)
      | eval total_limit_size=round(mem_metric*0.95,0)
      | eval queue_limit_length=128
      | eval q_len=0
      | eval output_p95_flush_latency_ms=0
      | eval retry_count_recent=0
      | eval environment=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(env,environment,"production"))))
      | fields _time host plugin_id output_id buffer_used_bytes total_limit_size queue_limit_length q_len output_p95_flush_latency_ms retry_count_recent environment ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (source=*splunktcpin* OR source=*SplunkTcp*)
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval plugin_id="splunk_tcpin_pressure"
      | eval output_id="hec_ingest_path"
      | eval buffer_used_bytes=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(current_queue_size,queue_size,kb,"0")),10)
      | eval total_limit_size=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(max_queue_size,queue_limit,"8000000")),10)
      | eval queue_limit_length=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(active_connections,connections,"0")),10)
      | eval q_len=queue_limit_length
      | eval output_p95_flush_latency_ms=tonumber(tostring(coalesce(wait_ms,latency_ms,"0")),10)
      | eval retry_count_recent=0
      | eval environment="platform_ingest"
      | fields _time host plugin_id output_id buffer_used_bytes total_limit_size queue_limit_length q_len output_p95_flush_latency_ms retry_count_recent environment ]
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval plugin_id=coalesce(plugin_id,"unknown_plugin")
| eval output_id=coalesce(output_id,"unknown_output")
| eval queue_length=coalesce(q_len,0)
| eval merge_key=strcat(host,"|",output_id,"|",plugin_id)
| sort 0 merge_key + _time
| streamstats window=30 current=t global=f first(_time) AS t0 first(buffer_used_bytes) AS v0 last(_time) AS t1 last(buffer_used_bytes) AS v1 BY merge_key
| eval delta_sec=coalesce(t1,0)-coalesce(t0,0)
| eval slope_bytes_per_min=if(delta_sec>1, round((coalesce(v1,0)-coalesce(v0,0))/(delta_sec/60),4), 0)
| eval buffer_used_bytes=coalesce(v1,buffer_used_bytes)
| eval buffer_used_pct=round(100*buffer_used_bytes/nullif(total_limit_size,0),3)
| eval buffer_headroom_bytes=coalesce(total_limit_size,0)-coalesce(buffer_used_bytes,0)
| eval slope_fill=case(slope_bytes_per_min>0,slope_bytes_per_min, true(),1)
| eval est_minutes_to_full=if(slope_bytes_per_min>0, round(buffer_headroom_bytes/slope_fill,2), 99999)
| streamstats window=5 current=t global=f sum(eval(if(buffer_used_pct>80 AND est_minutes_to_full<15,1,0))) AS sustain_hi BY merge_key
| eventstats perc95(buffer_used_bytes) AS env_buffer_p95 avg(buffer_used_bytes) AS env_buffer_avg BY environment
| sort 0 merge_key - _time
| dedup merge_key
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [| inputlookup fluentd_output_inventory.csv
     | eval output_id=trim(toString(coalesce(output_id,output_key,dest_code)))
     | fields output_id destination owner_team criticality on_call_runbook_url ]
| join type=left max=0 output_id
    [ search index=cmdb sourcetype=fluentd_pipeline_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
      | dedup output_id
      | eval output_id=trim(toString(output_id))
      | rename maint_window AS pipeline_maint_window
      | table output_id pipeline_region pipeline_maint_window ]
| eval destination=coalesce(destination,pipeline_region,"unmapped_destination")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval criticality=coalesce(criticality,"medium")
| eval severity=case(sustain_hi>=5 AND buffer_used_pct>80 AND est_minutes_to_full<15,"critical", buffer_used_pct>78 OR (est_minutes_to_full<20 AND slope_bytes_per_min>2048000),"high", buffer_used_pct>65 OR est_minutes_to_full<40 OR env_buffer_p95>buffer_used_bytes*0.85,"medium", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","page_oncall_expand_buffer_raise_flush_threads_validate_hec_token_and_disk_inode_budget", severity=="high","increase_flush_thread_count_shorten_flush_interval_trace_429_tls_and_chunk_limit_size", severity=="medium","schedule_capacity_review_rebalance_outputs_and_calibrate_exporter_cardinality", true(),"observe_trend_confirm_maintenance_windows_and_baseline_slopes")
| table _time, host, plugin_id, output_id, destination, owner_team, criticality, buffer_used_bytes, total_limit_size, buffer_used_pct, slope_bytes_per_min, est_minutes_to_full, queue_length, output_p95_flush_latency_ms, severity, recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_7_5_buffer_fill_forecast_alert]
cron_schedule = */10 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_7_5_buffer_fill_forecast | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives, materialize per-arm summaries hourly keyed on merge_key, widen alert windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on the node or pod. Compare Prometheus gauge values for buffer bytes and queue length to Splunk rows for the same output_id within one scrape interval. When numbers disagree, verify label rewriting, stale targets, and duplicated scrapers before blaming parsers.

Induce a controlled non-production slowdown on a Splunk HEC endpoint and confirm slope_bytes_per_min rises while retry_count_recent increments, with est_minutes_to_full falling below prior baselines. Restore service and confirm severity returns to low within two evaluation windows.

Fluent Bit validation: pause an output intentionally in lab, observe fluentbit_storage_chunks_busy_bytes growth in vendor tooling, and confirm the second arm mirrors the trend in Splunk.

Kubernetes validation: compare kubectl top pod memory to the fourth arm proxies for the same DaemonSet pod name. Large divergence implies field extraction drift.

Splunk tcpin validation: during indexer maintenance, confirm fifth arm rows do not page application teams when pipeline_maint_window annotations explain expected pressure.

Negative test: on a healthy cluster after drains complete, filter severity to critical and high and expect zero rows for thirty consecutive runs when thresholds match your noise floor.

Runbook linkage: document expected slope spikes during approved tenant onboarding when debug logging briefly elevates volume.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — chunk_limit_size set very large slows flush serialization and inflates stage bytes: reduce chunk size under change control, increase flush_thread_count cautiously, and validate network round trip time to Splunk HTTP Event Collector endpoints.

Case 2 — downstream HEC token throttled returning HTTP 429: split outputs, request higher quotas, validate load balancer health, and backoff using vendor retry settings without starving unrelated outputs on the same host.

Case 3 — output forwarder TLS handshake intermittent: inspect certificate rotation jobs, intermediate trust chains, and mutual TLS settings on ingress gateways; correlate with slope spikes that pause then resume.

Case 4 — DaemonSet memory limit too tight with imminent out-of-memory kill: raise limits after reviewing cgroup telemetry, reduce parser fan-out, and ensure quality-of-service classes protect logging pods.

Case 5 — sudden traffic spike from incident floods stdout: scale replicas, add sampling at trusted boundaries, and temporarily route noisy namespaces to cold storage sinks with governance approval.

Case 6 — tenant onboarded verbose debug logging in production: revert log levels, enforce policy guardrails in CI for helm values, and add budget alerts upstream of shipper buffers.

Case 7 — persistent volume backing filesystem buffer nears full disk: expand volumes, relocate storage.path, and prune orphaned chunks only under vendor-approved procedures.

Case 8 — flush_thread_count equal to one causes output serialization: raise threads with disk and CPU headroom, validate chunk creation rates, and watch for lock contention in custom formatters.

Case 9 — flush_interval too long combined with high write rate: shorten interval while monitoring CPU, pair with chunk_limit_size adjustments to avoid tiny chunk storms.

Case 10 — multiple outputs sharing the same buffer via copy routing: split buffers per destination, isolate retry policies, and ensure one slow sink cannot monopolize total_limit_size.

Case 11 — legacy td-agent 0.12 mixing with version one buffer semantics: migrate configurations explicitly, rebuild monitoring dashboards against current gauge names, and retire mixed fleets under a dated program.

Case 12 — gradual disk read latency degrading flush: move buffers to faster storage classes, measure fsync latency, and separate logging disks from database volumes.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, and join appear; final table lists sixteen analyst-facing columns including severity and recommended_action tokens aligned to inventory enrichment.
grandmaExplanation: We watch your log relay the way you watch a sink filling while the drain coughs: when the water line keeps rising faster than it drains, we estimate how many minutes remain before it kisses the overflow hole and we call for help early, long before water reaches the floor.
hardware: Linux virtual machines running Fluentd or td-agent under systemd, Kubernetes worker nodes running Fluent Bit or Splunk Connect for Kubernetes DaemonSets, and hybrid estates that include Calyptia enterprise builds forwarding to Splunk HTTP Event Collector endpoints.
